posts.author: Backboard5511 (Oliver)
posts.cookedHtml: Previously, in a view, changing the order of items would be restricted to that view. Now, it seems that changing the order of items in one view are being reflected other views. This did not use to be the case. Is this a bug, or a new feature?

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Backboard5511:

Previously, in a view, changing the order of items would be restricted to that view. Now, it seems that changing the order of items in one view are being reflected other views. This did not use to be the case. Is this a bug, or a new feature?

Hi @Backboard5511

Looks like monday now treats manual drag-and-drop ordering as the board’s main item order, rather than something isolated to a specific view.

So, if a view doesn’t have its own saved sort applied, it will reflect that updated board order which is likely why you’re seeing the changes across other views.

It doesn’t seem like a bug, but more of a shift in how item ordering is handled. If you’d like views to stay independent, applying and saving a specific Sort in each view should prevent that crossover.
